implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: Intent matches the spec exactly — footer text, positioning, and styling are all correct — but the implementation introduces a JSX compile error by placing <footer> as a sibling of <main> without a fragment wrapper (<> … </>). The file won't build as written, so none of the acceptance criteria can be verified at runtime. The fix is one line (add <> / </>) but the bug is hard to miss in review.

requirements.notes: Footer text '© 2026 BrewBoard', placement after main content, and the one-file change all match the spec. Year is hardcoded to 2026 (technically correct on 2026-05-11 but not dynamic). The footer won't render because the file won't compile, so acceptance criteria cannot be validated as shipped.

correctness.notes: Critical JSX bug: <footer> is a direct sibling of <main> inside the return () without a React Fragment wrapper. Next.js/React requires a single root element (or <>…</>). The file will fail to compile with 'Adjacent JSX elements must be wrapped in an enclosing tag'. All other JSX and Tailwind classes are correct.

minimality.notes: 3 lines of production code added, no existing code touched, no unnecessary imports or refactors. Perfectly scoped.

code_quality.notes: Tailwind classes are idiomatic and consistent with the rest of the file. Inner text is over-indented by 4 spaces relative to the opening tag. Hardcoded '2026' rather than new Date().getFullYear() is a minor quality miss given the spec asked for 'current year'. Fragment omission is the dominant issue.

risk.notes: Broken build is the highest-severity risk — the entire app fails to render, not just the footer. Secondary concern: text-stone-500 on a white background is #78716c, which passes AA for large text but is borderline for normal text (contrast ratio ~4.6:1). Fragment fix is trivial but the build breakage makes this a blocking defect.
